ideas.title: Non-stationary, covariate-informed geostatistical interpolation for robust validation surfaces
ideas.description: This approach creates continuous validation surfaces by moving beyond traditional stationary kriging. It first decomposes the spatial field into a deterministic component, modeled via machine learning using high-resolution spatial covariates, and a stochastic residual component. A non-stationary geostatistical model, whose parameters vary as a function of location and landscape characteristics, is then applied to interpolate the residuals. This hybrid system directly accounts for spatial heterogeneity, producing more accurate interpolation in complex terrain and providing spatially explicit estimates of prediction uncertainty that reflect local variability.

ideas.title: Interactive, Bias-Corrected Environmental Data Portals with Explainable AI for Non-Specialists
ideas.description: This project develops an accessible web-based platform that serves machine learning-corrected environmental data products, such as bias-corrected MODIS Net Primary Productivity (NPP). The core innovation is integrating explainable AI (XAI) techniques directly into the user interface, allowing non-specialists to visualize original data, corrected data, and spatially explicit explanations of the model's bias corrections (e.g., highlighting areas where cloud cover or topography caused the largest adjustments). The platform will feature intuitive tools for scenario exploration, plain-language documentation of the correction process, and guided workflows, empowering educators, land managers, and policy analysts to confidently use high-quality, research-grade data without needing expertise in remote sensing or machine learning.

ideas.title: Employ Bayesian Kriging with a Geophysical Prior for Validation Interpolation
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from marine geology, where Bayesian kriging integrates sparse, precise core samples with continuous geophysical data to map seabed sediments. The analogous problem in validation site interpolation is creating continuous surfaces (e.g., for net ecosystem exchange) from sparse tower network measurements. The adapted method uses continuous remote sensing products as a geostatistical prior. Bayesian kriging then formally updates this prior with the tower measurements, producing a posterior probability map of the flux variable that has reduced uncertainty compared to interpolation from towers alone, effectively fusing different data qualities and resolutions for superior validation surfaces.

ideas.title: A Hybrid Data-Model Fusion System for Detecting and Correcting Structural Errors in GPP Models
ideas.description: This research develops a process-aware hybrid data assimilation framework to overcome structural errors in remote sensing GPP models. The system uses an Ensemble Kalman Filter to assimilate flux tower GPP data while monitoring innovation sequences to diagnose where and when the model physics fail. It integrates a modular model environment with alternative representations for critical processes (e.g., light use efficiency, stomatal conductance). The assimilation then operates in a dual state-parameter-proposal space, not only adjusting parameters but also probabilistically weighting competing process formulations to dynamically improve the model's core structure.

ideas.title: Downscale MODIS NPP via Super-Resolution Convolutional Neural Networks Adapted from Medical Imaging
ideas.description: This idea adapts a super-resolution solution from medical imaging to the problem of scaling MODIS Net Primary Productivity (NPP). In radiology, Super-Resolution Convolutional Neural Networks (SRCNNs) successfully enhance low-resolution MRI/CT scans by learning from paired high-resolution images. The adapted method involves training a SRCNN model using paired datasets where 1-km MODIS NPP estimates (low-resolution target) are aligned with 30-m Landsat surface reflectance data (high-resolution input features). The model learns the complex, non-linear spectral-spatial relationships, allowing it to downscale MODIS NPP to a 30-meter resolution, effectively fusing the frequent, global MODIS coverage with the fine spatial detail of Landsat.

ideas.title: Adapting Bioinformatics Visualization Suites for Domain-Specific Data Exploration
ideas.description: Drawing an analogy from bioinformatics, where platforms like the UCSC Genome Browser successfully made vast genomic data accessible to non-specialist researchers, this research proposes adapting that solution to other scientific domains. The analogous solution involved creating interactive, graphical web interfaces that layered information visually and provided built-in tools, thereby hiding command-line complexity. The adapted idea is to develop domain-specific 'data explorer' platforms for fields like climate science or materials chemistry. These platforms would visually structure complex datasets onto intuitive frameworks (e.g., maps, timelines), integrate point-and-click analytical tools, and embed contextual documentation and tutorials directly within the user interface to guide non-specialist users through data access and preliminary exploration.

ideas.title: Context-Aware Data Literacy Scaffolding for Robust Non-Specialist Interpretation
ideas.description: This research develops a system that moves beyond interface simplification to directly build the user's conceptual understanding during data exploration. It dynamically assesses a user's implicit queries and knowledge state to inject explanatory scaffolds, corrective feedback, and analogy-based framing into the data interaction loop. The goal is to create tools that are not just usable but also pedagogically effective, transforming data interaction into a learning experience that reduces misinterpretation risks. This results in a more robust form of accessibility where users gain literacy as they use the system, enabling truly informed analysis.
